text_x: Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev's X Account Suspected Compromised, VLAD Token Exploiter Gains $1.2M–$1.3M Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev's X account was suspected to have been compromised after it posted an unusual message on July 23, 2026, claiming to launch Robinhood Chain's "official" mascot token Vladhood (VLAD) and sharing a token contract address. The token contract was later marked as "SCAM" on the Robinhood Chain block explorer, warning users of potential fraud risks. The post has since been deleted. According to on-chain monitoring by MLM, the attacker generated around 650 ETH in proceeds from the VLAD token, worth approximately $1.2 million to $1.3 million. — link

text_x: Maybe Intelligence Ain't All That AI hit a ceiling not because it lacks intelligence, but because most hard problems require contact with reality, not just better reasoning. > The takeoff story assumed the limit was thinking. It isn't. The limit is contact with reality. > A smarter reasoner fills the gaps between known facts in simple areas faster and better, but it doesn't produce new facts. > Coming up with ideas was never the hard part. The hard part is how fast reality answers them. Source:

text_x: Error regarding my thesis $FEFER. I had the impression that Stable was publicly positioned as Tethers chain when it's only backed by Tether even though it's technically "on the books". The chain having a token made the trade feel less asymmetric as well as it puts a roof over its head in terms of comparisons etc. Now there's pvp on the token. It's still valued at 1 bil mcap and 4 bil FDV, although the dynamics of already being live for a while with hardly anything built on top of it makes it feel like they're just chasing the recent meme hype to pump metrics. I kept getting more and more bullish until I had to check with friends what I was missing. Very important to have solid people around you who can point out the bear cases. Apologies on my part for the bad research, will take more care next time and might be time to take a break.

text_x: Kazakhstan Introduces Strategic Mining Rules, Expanding State Crypto Reserve Plan Kazakhstan approved the "Strategic Digital Mining Implementation Rules" on July 18, 2026. Under the rules, strategic digital mining companies can receive electricity quotas at rates capped by the maximum price for power producers, but must transfer part of their mined digital assets to Astana Hub. The assets will be managed by a state investment company under the National Bank of Kazakhstan and used for the country's strategic crypto reserve. The reserve may invest in digital assets, digital asset-backed derivatives, and equity of companies involved in digital asset development or investment. The rules will take effect 10 calendar days after official publication. — link

text_x: Ten Questions and Answers: A Comprehensive Guide to Robinhood Chain Robinhood Chain is an Ethereum Layer 2 launched by Robinhood in July 2026 and built with Arbitrum technology. The network is designed for tokenized real-world assets (RWAs), including Stock Tokens and ETFs, as well as DeFi, perpetual futures, and AI-agent applications. According to a Bernstein report, Robinhood Chain recorded approximately $3.1 billion in DEX trading volume over a seven-day period, with around $300 million in stablecoins and $13 million in Stock Tokens. However, much of the early activity came from memecoin trading, while RWA applications remained at an early stage. This guide explains Robinhood Chain's architecture, including its Arbitrum-based design, ETH gas model, EVM compatibility, wallet access, and bridging process. It also examines Stock Tokens, which provide economic exposure to stocks but do not represent direct ownership or shareholder rights, along with key risks related to regulation, liquidity, infrastructure centralization, bridges, smart contracts, and self-custody. — link

text_x: Bought $FEFER on Stable's (Tethers) new chain. The first meme on Stable and pushed by Paolo (CEO) multiple times and retweeted by Stable chain. Currently the main runner and would assume it to stay that way considering the CEO and Stable both tweeted it today. No infra yet, coins are less than 24 hours old, bridges are slow and failing. Txns are also quite slow and failing due to slippage and low liq. To bridge you can use Relay and then buy through DYORswap: Tethers mcap is 180 billion, 10 billion in revenue last year. Robinhoods is 90 billion mcap, 4.5 billion revenue this past year. The two companies are very different and their user bases/potential new customers aren't the same, except there's enough asymmetry to gamble on especially being in the first 24 hours with direct endorsement imo. Very high risk -> very high reward. Wouldn't size these coins too crazy but if you think it's a good narrative nows the time to play. 8 mil is a decent RR for myself.

text_x: Strategy Launches Bitcoin Security Consortium With BlackRock, Coinbase and Others, Pledging $15 Million Over Three Years Strategy has launched the Bitcoin Security Consortium, with founding members including BlackRock, Coinbase, Galaxy, Anchorage, ARK Invest, Blockstream, Block, and Digital Asset. The members have collectively pledged $15 million over the next three years to support Bitcoin developers and security research. The consortium said its first area of focus will be preparing for the long-term implications of quantum computing on Bitcoin cryptography, while emphasizing that it will not direct Bitcoin's development but instead support the existing open-source developer and research community. — link

text_x: EU Agrees New Russia Sanctions, Expands Restrictions on Crypto Platforms According to Reuters, EU member state envoys have agreed on the 21st sanctions package against Russia. The measures place 94 Russian financial institutions and the Moscow Exchange under full sanctions, expand transaction bans on additional crypto platforms, and for the first time target vessels supporting Russia's shadow fleet. The package also freezes the Russian oil price cap at $44.10 per barrel for 12 months. — link

text_x: QCP Macro Themes - 23 July 2026 Alphabet beat on cloud and search, and still fell 4% after-hours. The problem was not the quarter but the spending behind it, with 2026 AI capex lifted to $195-205bn reviving margin-durability concerns. That is the tension running through the tape. TSMC delivered Q2 revenue at the top of guidance and ASML raised its 2026 outlook, so the semis momentum is real. But the market is no longer rewarding the capex that drives it, with Tesla and Intel reporting next. Broader risk stays pressured by the widening Gulf conflict. Hormuz traffic has collapsed to a near standstill, and the SPR has drawn down to its lowest since 1983, leaving little cushion if disruption forces further releases. If a beat is not enough, what does the AI trade need to prove next? Read the full Macro Themes here.

text_x: Breaking: Arthur Hayes-Founded BitMEX, Pioneer of Crypto Perpetuals, to Shut Down BitMEX said it will permanently close its exchange at 04:00 UTC on September 23 and has stopped accepting new user registrations. Co-founded by Arthur Hayes in 2014, BitMEX introduced the crypto industry's first perpetual swap and was once one of the world's dominant derivatives exchanges before losing market share to Binance and Bybit. Hayes and his co-founders stepped down after U.S. criminal charges in 2020, while a reported effort to sell the exchange in 2025 did not result in an announced deal. — link

text_x: Verus–Ethereum Bridge Suffers Second Exploit in Two Months, $7.54M Drained Blockaid detected a new exploit targeting the Verus–Ethereum Bridge, with an attacker abusing the bridge's import path to trigger unbacked Ethereum-side payouts and drain approximately $7.54 million in ETH, tBTC, USDC, USDT, EURC, MKR and scrvUSD. The stolen assets were subsequently converted into ETH. Blockaid said the latest attack involved the same bridge contract, entry path and vulnerability class as the approximately $11.5 million exploit in May, but was carried out by a different attacker using a new wallet. During the previous incident on May 18, the stolen assets were converted into approximately 5,402.4 ETH. The attacker later returned 4,052.4 ETH after retaining a 25% white-hat bounty, and the returned funds were redeposited into the bridge on July 8. — link

text_x: this is how i trade. what i do is scalping attention. that is my job. believing in smth is not my job. mapping out possibilities of exchange listings, crime team twaps or industry leaders liking tweets is not my job. my work is gauging if smth is capable of achieving a certain attention threshold. if the verdict is yes and said attention threshold is not reached yet, i am buying. when the attention threshold is reached i am selling. that does not involve speculating if peak attention is reached. example is my $AI trade today. i bought when there was not much talk about it yesterday and i sold when half the timeline talked about it today. the attention threshold is reached. my work is done. my edge is exhausted. can it go much higher from here? absolutely. one Vlad twitter interaction will send it flying, but i have no edge in mapping out possibilities of such an event occurring. that is not my job. i am not in the business of dreaming and i am not in the business of gauging ceilings. i am in the business of scalping attention. and by doing this it is only natural that from time to time i will sell an asset that does many multiples after my exit. and that is totally fine. i do not feel pain about it anymore(i did in the past, and it is a hard habit to get rid off). i acknowledge that selling some runners too early is part of my job. it is part of the contract i signed and it is necessary and crucial for the environment where i can do my job to exist in the first place. if dreamers would not be rewarded from time to time there would be nothing to scalp for me. its a symbiotic relationship. dreamers must stomach frequent roundtrips and getting their lunch eaten by scalpers in order to catch a huge multiple from time to time, and scalpers must accept selling a giga runner too early from time to time in exchange for no roundtrips. these are the contracts we signed.
author: admin#2325165654

text_x: AI Spending Pushes Alphabet to First-Ever Negative Free Cash Flow Alphabet's second-quarter capital spending doubled from a year earlier to USD 44.92 billion, pushing quarterly free cash flow to a record-low negative USD 5.86 billion—the company's first-ever negative reading. Google Cloud revenue surged 81.8% to USD 24.77 billion and beat expectations, but Alphabet raised its full-year capital expenditure guidance again, sending its shares down about 3% in after-hours trading. — link

text_x: Ether ETFs Outpace Bitcoin Funds as BlackRock Leads Inflows According to SoSoValue, U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded total net inflows of USD 68.99 million on July 23, led by BlackRock's IBIT with USD 38.78 million. Spot Ethereum ETFs drew USD 72.64 million, with BlackRock's ETHA posting the largest single-day inflow at USD 53.47 million. BlackRock is the world's largest asset manager and operates the largest U.S. spot Bitcoin ETF by assets. — link

text_x: Grayscale: Bitcoin Bear Market Could Last Until September or October Grayscale said Bitcoin's current bear market could continue until September or October if the traditional four-year cycle holds, as previous downturns typically bottomed about a year after the market peak and produced average drawdowns of roughly 80%. However, the firm favors a macro-driven framework, arguing that Bitcoin is becoming a more mature asset whose performance increasingly depends on economic growth and real interest rates. Grayscale said Bitcoin may have already bottomed if the U.S. economy remains resilient and the Federal Reserve avoids further rate increases. — link

text_x: Grayscale Filing Shows 100 Wallets Hold 90% of Circulating Worldcoin Grayscale's S-1 filing for a proposed Worldcoin ETF shows that the 100 largest WLD wallets held about 90% of the token's circulating supply as of the filing date, although some addresses, including cross-chain bridge wallets, may aggregate assets belonging to multiple users. WLD is the native token of World Network, the digital identity and financial network initially developed by Sam Altman, the co-founder and CEO of OpenAI. — link

text_x: Japan Could Launch Its First Bitcoin ETF as Early as 2028 Japan could introduce its first Bitcoin exchange-traded fund as early as 2028, as the Financial Services Agency prepares to revise investment-fund rules following legislation that brings crypto assets under the Financial Instruments and Exchange Act. Several major asset managers are considering entering the market, while some estimates suggest Japanese Bitcoin ETFs could attract up to JPY 3 trillion by fiscal 2028. — link
